title: not possible to get fedora image for e2e testing
body:

comments.body: not much we can do about that? ideas?
This looks like the Fedora registry being its usual, very flaky self. I would try again in a few hours and see if it works.
ok so it seems that works with podman version 1.8.0, which is weird ...
We actually changed the location we fetch images from on Master to avoid the Fedora registries due to issues like this
That patch might be backportable - there's no real changes aside from where the image we use for the tests lives.
@mheon thanks for all the help , I will close this issue :)

Has podman 1.8.0 or earlier added support to wildcard SSL certificates? With 1.4.2 I get similar wildcard error:

Error: error pulling image "my-test-image:latest": unable to pull my-test-image:latest: 1 error occurred:
        * Error initializing source docker://docker-virtual.production.artifactory.example.com/my-test-image:latest: pinging docker registry returned: Get https://docker-virtual.production.artifactory.example.com/v2/: x509: certificate is valid for artifactory.example.com, *.artifactory.example.com, not docker-virtual.production.artifactory.example.com
